name: HOUGHALL SHAFT

surface_od_ft.qualifier: as written: 134' O.D. (final digit smudged; 13.25 ruled out — would sit below the Wear floodplain datum at Houghall)

units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: depth 5-1-6 is underlined in the original depth column (subtotal rule) — marks the base of the superficial drift / rockhead
first bedrock below drift; 'metal' = argillaceous mudstone
seam named in original ink: COAL (Low main)
transitional to sandstone (post) per the original description
top leaf of the basal coal (with i=18 'COAL, bottom')
bottom leaf of the basal coal

seam_marks.how: pencil marginalia: 'D2M' (verbatim; abbreviation not in the provided legend, left unexpanded)
pencil marginalia: 'TBT' (verbatim; not in provided legend — likely Top Brass Thill leaf, being the upper of two thin coals bracketing the thill stone at i=10)
pencil marginalia: 'BBT' (verbatim; not in provided legend — likely Bottom Brass Thill leaf, the lower thin coal below the thill stone)
pencil marginalia: 'Hn' (verbatim; cf. legend HTN = Hutton)

notes: Page 1 carries the entire strata section; page 2 is a blank ruled continuation sheet (Thickness / Depth-from-Surface column headers only, no strata). Full arithmetic chain verified in inches: every one of the 18 Depth-from-Surface entries equals the running sum of Thickness exactly — no clerk slips anywhere in the log. (Terminal depth 31 fa 3 ft 5 in = 2273 in = 57.73 m, vs BGS index 57.62 m; the index figure ~matches the base of the 'good' coal leaf at 31-3-1 = 57.61 m.) Document title reads 'Section of Strata in Houghall Estate' (BGS index name: HOUGHALL SHAFT). Header: Surface Level 134' O.D. (final digit smudged), Date of boring or sinking 1841, One-inch Map 27, Six-inch Map Durham 27 (NW); 'Communicated by' reference reads approximately 'B.A.S. 1189'. Pencil seam correlations by a later BGS geologist: D2M against the Low Main coal (i=7), TBT and BBT against the two thin coals bracketing the thill seatearth (i=9, i=11 — likely Top/Bottom Brass Thill leaves), and Hn against the basal coal (i=18 — cf. HTN=Hutton). All pencil abbreviations recorded verbatim; only the Hn/Hutton cue matches the provided legend. BGS blue banner, watermarks and registration stamp ignored as non-log content.
